main_app.api_views module¶

main_app.api_views.api_approve_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows an editor to approve an article

main_app.api_views.api_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Displays articles to an approved user

main_app.api_views.api_create_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist to create an article

main_app.api_views.api_create_newsletter(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist to create a new newsletter

main_app.api_views.api_delete_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist or editor to delete an article

main_app.api_views.api_delete_newsletter(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist or editor to delete a newsletter

main_app.api_views.api_newsletters(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Returns all newsletter

main_app.api_views.api_single_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Displays a single article to an approved user

main_app.api_views.api_single_newsletter(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Returns a single newsletter

main_app.api_views.api_subscribed_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Displays a list of a reader’s subscribed articles

main_app.api_views.api_update_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist or editor to edit an article

main_app.api_views.api_update_newsletter(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allows a journalist or editor to update a newsletter

Represents a news article

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

class main_app.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Model for custom user to allocate groups and subscriptions

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

REQUIRED_FIELDS = ['email', 'role']¶
ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor')]¶
USERNAME_FIELD = 'username'¶
article_set¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

role¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Save the current instance. Override this in a subclass if you want to control the saving process.

The ‘force_insert’ and ‘force_update’ parameters can be used to insist that the “save” must be an SQL insert or update (or equivalent for non-SQL backends), respectively. Normally, they should not be set.

main_app.views module¶

main_app.views.apply_editor(request, publisher_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to apply to a publisher

main_app.views.apply_journalist(request, publisher_id)[source]¶
main_app.views.approve_article(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows editors to approve an article

main_app.views.approve_editor(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to approve an editor to a publisher

main_app.views.approve_journalist(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to approve a journalist to a publisher

main_app.views.article_catalogue(request)[source]¶

Displays all articles

main_app.views.create_article(request)[source]¶

Allows a journalist to create an article

main_app.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]¶

Allows journalists to create a newsletter

main_app.views.create_publisher(request)[source]¶

Allows an editor to create a publisher

main_app.views.delete_article(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows journalists and editors to delete articles

main_app.views.delete_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Allows journalists and editors to delete a newsletter

main_app.views.delete_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to delete a publisher

main_app.views.edit_article(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows journalists and editors to edit articles

main_app.views.edit_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Allows journalists and editors to edit a newsletter

main_app.views.is_editor(user)[source]¶
main_app.views.is_journalist(user)[source]¶
main_app.views.is_reader(user)[source]¶
main_app.views.journalist_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a list of all journalists

main_app.views.manage_articles(request)[source]¶

Allows editors to manage articles

main_app.views.manage_newsletters(request)[source]¶

Allows users to manage newsletters

main_app.views.manage_publishers(request)[source]¶
main_app.views.my_articles(request)[source]¶

Allows a journalist to view their own articles

main_app.views.my_newsletters(request)[source]¶

Allows a journalist to view their own newsletters

main_app.views.my_subscriptions(request)[source]¶

Displays a list of a reader’s subscriptions to them

main_app.views.newsletter_catalogue(request)[source]¶
main_app.views.publisher_list(request)[source]¶

Displays a list of all publishers

main_app.views.reject_editor(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to reject an editor applying to a publisher

main_app.views.reject_journalist(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to reject a journalist applying to a publisher

main_app.views.remove_editor(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to remove an editor from a publisher

main_app.views.remove_journalist(request, publisher_id, user_id)[source]¶

Allows an editor to remove a journalist from a publisher

main_app.views.subscribe_journalist(request, journalist_id)[source]¶

Allows a reader to subscribe to a journalist

main_app.views.subscribe_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]¶

Allows a reader to subscribe to a publisher

main_app.views.unsubscribe_journalist(request, journalist_id)[source]¶

Allows a reader to unsubscribe to a journalist

main_app.views.unsubscribe_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]¶

Allows a reader to unsubscribe to a publisher

main_app.views.view_article(request, article_id)[source]¶

Allows users to view a specific article

main_app.views.view_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Allows a user to view a newsletter
